hero.headline: Build AI systems that deliver measurable business outcomes
hero.subheadline: From machine learning to generative AI — we design, deploy, and scale production-grade AI for financial services, healthcare, and government organizations that demand accuracy, explainability, and regulatory compliance.

challenge.title: Most AI pilots never reach production
challenge.description: According to Gartner, over 80% of AI projects fail to deliver business value. The gap isn't ideas — it's engineering rigour, data readiness, and operational maturity.
painPoints: AI pilots demonstrate promise but stall in production due to fragile data pipelines and manual deployment
Regulated industries require explainable models, audit trails, and bias testing — rarely built into pilots
Model drift goes undetected without proper monitoring, degrading accuracy silently over months
Integration with legacy systems (core banking, EMR, case management) is treated as an afterthought
Business stakeholders can't distinguish high-impact use cases from interesting-but-unused technical demos

howWeDeliver.title: How We Deliver AI Solutions
howWeDeliver.description: We take a practical, business-first approach to AI — engineering for production from day one, not bolting it on after the pilot.
items: Identify high-impact AI opportunities grounded in measurable KPIs
Build and train custom machine learning models with explainability built in
Integrate AI into your existing systems and workflows (not siloed demos)
Deploy scalable, production-ready solutions with MLOps automation
Continuously monitor model drift and improve performance post-launch
Establish governance frameworks for responsible, compliant AI

keyUseCases.title: Key Use Cases
keyUseCases.description: We help organizations apply AI where it creates real value:
items: Predict customer behavior and reduce churn
Automate customer support with AI assistants
Detect fraud and anomalies in real time
Extract insights from documents and unstructured data
Analyze images and video for quality and security
Personalize products, services, and user experiences

businessImpact.title: Business Impact
businessImpact.description: Our AI solutions are designed to deliver measurable outcomes — not vanity metrics.
items: Faster, data-driven decision-making at every level
Reduced costs and risks with predictive intervention
Competitive advantage through proprietary data assets
Regulatory readiness with auditable model governance

points.title: Engineering Discipline
points.description: We bring software engineering rigour to data science — version control, testing, CI/CD, and observability for models from day one.

points.title: Production-First Mindset
points.description: We don't build Jupyter notebook demos. Every model we build is designed to run in production, at scale, with monitoring.

points.title: Regulated Industry Expertise
points.description: Deep understanding of OSFI, PIPEDA, PHIPA and the constraints that shape AI in regulated sectors.

points.title: Explainability by Default
points.description: Every model ships with interpretability tools, bias testing, and audit trails — essentials for regulated deployment.

caseStudy.title: From Fraud Pilot to Production Platform
caseStudy.challenge: A fraud detection pilot had been stuck in proof-of-concept for 18 months. Model accuracy was acceptable, but deployment was manual, explanations couldn't be generated for disputed transactions, and drift was undetected.
caseStudy.approach: We rebuilt the pipeline on MLOps foundations — automated training, SHAP-based explanations, real-time scoring infrastructure, and drift monitoring dashboards. Partnered with risk and compliance teams throughout.
caseStudy.result: Production deployment achieved in 4 months. False-positive rate dropped 40%, investigator workload reduced, and every flagged transaction now ships with a regulator-ready explanation.

items.question: How do you measure AI project success?
items.answer: We tie every AI initiative to specific business KPIs before writing code — reduction in false positives, improvement in forecast accuracy, dollars saved, customer retention uplift. Technical metrics (F1, ROC-AUC) matter only in service of these business outcomes.

items.question: Can you work with our existing data infrastructure?
items.answer: Yes. We regularly integrate with legacy data warehouses, cloud data platforms, EMRs, core banking systems, and custom applications. Our role is to make AI work within your environment, not demand a greenfield rebuild.

items.question: How do you ensure models remain compliant with Canadian regulations?
items.answer: We build explainability, bias testing, and audit trails into every model from day one. Our work aligns with OSFI model risk management guidelines, PIPEDA/PHIPA requirements, and emerging federal AI and Data Act (AIDA) standards.

items.question: Do you use generative AI or focus on traditional ML?
items.answer: Both — the right tool for the job. Generative AI excels at document processing, customer support, and content tasks. Traditional ML often wins for structured-data forecasting, fraud detection, and credit scoring where explainability is paramount.

items.question: What happens after the model is deployed?
items.answer: Deployment is the beginning, not the end. We establish monitoring for model drift, data quality degradation, and performance decay — plus a clear retraining cadence. Many of our longest client relationships are ongoing model operations.

closing.title: Ready to unlock the power of AI in your organization?
closing.subtitle: Let's discuss how we can help you identify and implement high-impact AI solutions that actually ship.

painPoints: Data lives in dozens of disconnected systems — no single source of truth
Reports from different teams contradict each other because definitions drift
Legacy data warehouses can't handle modern volume, variety, or velocity
Data quality issues surface as broken dashboards and failed AI models
Compliance (PIPEDA, PHIPA, OSFI) requires lineage you can't produce

Ensure high data quality, reliability, and consistency
Enable seamless access for analytics, BI, and AI use cases
Establish data governance, lineage, and cataloging from day one

differentiators.title: Why Gamma Neutral for Data Engineering

points.title: Architecture That Lasts
points.description: We design data platforms to evolve — modular, cloud-native architectures that accommodate new sources, volumes, and use cases without rebuilds.

points.title: Governance Built In, Not Bolted On
points.description: Lineage, cataloging, quality, and access controls are part of the foundation — not a phase 2 afterthought.

points.title: Reliability as a Discipline
points.description: Pipeline observability, SLA monitoring, and automated recovery are standard in every platform we build.

points.title: Regulated-Industry Proven
points.description: Deep experience with BCBS 239, PHIPA, PIPEDA, OSFI, and the audit requirements that shape Canadian data engineering.

caseStudy.title: From Siloed Reports to Single Source of Truth
caseStudy.challenge: 12 different departments maintained their own data extracts from the EMR. Reports never matched, analytics took weeks, and no one could trace data back to its clinical origin — blocking PHIPA audit requests.
caseStudy.approach: We designed a lakehouse platform with HL7/FHIR ingestion, a canonical clinical data model, automated quality monitoring, and full lineage tracking from EMR to dashboard.
caseStudy.result: Single source of truth for 40+ KPIs in 6 months. Audit responses completed in hours instead of weeks. Analytics team shifted from data wrangling to actual analysis.

items.question: Do we need to migrate off our legacy data warehouse?
items.answer: Not necessarily. We often modernize incrementally — adding a cloud data lake/lakehouse alongside existing warehouses, then migrating workloads as value is proven. Big-bang migrations rarely end well.

items.question: How do you handle data quality?
items.answer: We treat data quality as a first-class engineering concern: automated expectations testing (Great Expectations), freshness monitoring, schema drift detection, and anomaly alerts wired into your observability stack.

items.question: What's the difference between a data lake, warehouse, and lakehouse?
items.answer: Data warehouses excel at structured analytics (think Snowflake). Data lakes store raw/semi-structured data cheaply. Lakehouses (Databricks, Iceberg) combine both — structured analytics performance on open storage. We pick the right fit for your workloads.

items.question: Can you work with our on-prem data?
items.answer: Yes. We design hybrid architectures where sensitive data stays on-prem and analytical workloads run in the cloud, with secure connectivity and governance across both environments.

items.question: How long does a data platform implementation take?
items.answer: A production-ready platform typically takes 3-6 months for initial rollout, then iterative expansion. We prioritize delivering value early with a 'walking skeleton' approach rather than 12-month waterfall programs.

closing.title: Ready to build a modern data platform that scales with your business?
closing.subtitle: Let's design a data architecture that turns your data into a strategic asset.

hero.headline: Scale infrastructure with performance, security, and cost discipline
hero.subheadline: From multi-cloud architecture to application modernization — we help Canadian organizations adopt cloud strategically, aligned with OSFI B-10 guidelines, PHIPA, and government cloud-first policies.

challenge.title: Cloud promises are easy. Cloud reality is hard.
challenge.description: Cloud adoption should deliver agility, scalability, and efficiency — yet many Canadian organizations end up with runaway costs, security concerns, and half-migrated workloads that work worse than what they replaced.
painPoints: Cloud bills grow faster than the business — no FinOps discipline to reign them in
Lift-and-shift migrations preserve legacy problems in more expensive infrastructure
Shadow IT emerges when developers can't get fast, secure cloud provisioning
OSFI B-10 and data residency requirements weren't fully considered upfront
Without IaC and CI/CD, environments drift and releases become risky events

howWeDeliver.title: How We Deliver Cloud Solutions

Continuously optimize performance, reliability, and costs
Establish FinOps practices to keep spending aligned with value

items: Strong foundation for data and AI initiatives

points.title: Canadian Compliance Expertise
points.description: Deep familiarity with OSFI B-10, PHIPA, PIPEDA, GC cloud policy, and the Canadian regulatory landscape.

points.title: FinOps from Day One
points.description: Cost discipline built into architecture decisions — not a cleanup project after the bills shock you.

points.title: Multi-Cloud Reality
points.description: We work across AWS, Azure, and GCP based on what fits your workloads — not vendor allegiance.

points.title: Platform Engineering Mindset
points.description: We build internal developer platforms that make cloud self-service safe — empowering dev teams while maintaining guardrails.

caseStudy.title: Lift, Shift, and Modernize — Without the Bill Shock
caseStudy.challenge: Legacy claims processing ran on aging on-prem infrastructure. A previous cloud migration had doubled hosting costs with minimal agility gains. Leadership demanded both OSFI compliance and cost reduction.
caseStudy.approach: We rearchitected the workload using AWS landing zones, Kubernetes for stateless services, and managed databases. Implemented Terraform, GitOps, and FinOps dashboards with automated rightsizing.
caseStudy.result: 35% infrastructure cost reduction vs. original cloud migration. Release frequency up from monthly to daily. Full OSFI B-10 compliance achieved with automated evidence collection.

items.question: Should we go multi-cloud or stick with one provider?
items.answer: It depends. Single-cloud delivers simpler operations and deeper integration. Multi-cloud offers vendor resilience and best-of-breed services. We help evaluate the trade-offs honestly based on your risk tolerance and team capacity.

items.question: How do you handle Canadian data residency requirements?
items.answer: All major cloud providers offer Canadian regions (ca-central-1, Canada East/Central). We design architectures that enforce data residency through IAM policies, network controls, and automated compliance checks.

items.question: What's your approach to cloud cost control?
items.answer: FinOps from the architecture phase — rightsizing, reserved capacity planning, lifecycle policies, and cost allocation tagging built in. Plus dashboards that make spend visible to the teams driving it.

items.question: Can you help us modernize without a full rewrite?
items.answer: Yes. We follow the 6R framework (rehost, replatform, refactor, repurchase, retain, retire) — matching modernization depth to business value, not applying one approach to everything.

items.question: What about compliance evidence for audits?
items.answer: We implement automated compliance frameworks (AWS Config, Azure Policy, GCP Policy Intelligence) that continuously enforce controls and generate audit evidence — critical for OSFI, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 readiness.

closing.title: Ready to modernize your infrastructure and accelerate your cloud journey?
closing.subtitle: Let's design a cloud strategy tailored to your business goals and regulatory environment.

hero.headline: Protect your business against evolving cyber threats
hero.subheadline: We design and implement enterprise-grade security programs for Canadian organizations in financial services, healthcare, and government — aligned with OSFI, PIPEDA, PHIPA, SOC 2, and NIST frameworks.

challenge.title: Cyber threats outpace traditional defenses
challenge.description: Canadian organizations face ransomware, supply-chain attacks, and insider threats on a weekly basis — while regulators demand stronger controls and faster breach notification. Legacy perimeter defenses no longer suffice.
painPoints: Ransomware and supply-chain attacks target Canadian SMBs, healthcare, and municipalities relentlessly
Cloud expansion widens the attack surface faster than security teams can keep up
OSFI, PIPEDA, PHIPA — plus sector guidelines — create overlapping compliance obligations
Board and leadership demand cyber risk quantification most teams can't provide
Incident response plans exist on paper but break down in real events

howWeDeliver.title: How We Secure Your Organization
howWeDeliver.description: We take a proactive, risk-based approach to cybersecurity — protecting your systems, data, and operations against evolving threats.
items: Assess your current security posture and identify vulnerabilities
Design and implement enterprise-grade security architectures
Deploy advanced threat detection and response capabilities
Establish continuous monitoring and incident response processes
Ensure compliance with industry standards and regulations
Build security awareness through training and tabletop exercises

keyUseCases.title: Key Use Cases
keyUseCases.description: We help organizations strengthen security across critical areas:
items: Detect and respond to cyber threats in real time
Protect sensitive data and customer information

ourApproach.title: Our Approach
ourApproach.description: We cover the full cybersecurity lifecycle from assessment through continuous improvement.

items.title: Security Assessment & Risk Analysis
items.description: Current-state evaluation against frameworks like NIST CSF, ISO 27001, or CIS Controls — with risk quantification for the board.

items.title: Security Architecture & Controls Implementation
items.description: Design and deploy technical and administrative controls aligned to your risk tolerance and regulatory obligations.

items.title: Threat Detection & Incident Response
items.description: Stand up SIEM/SOAR capabilities, define runbooks, and conduct tabletop exercises so your team is ready when it matters.

items.title: Identity & Access Management (IAM)
items.description: Implement zero-trust principles: least-privilege access, MFA everywhere, PAM for admins, and lifecycle governance.

items.title: Compliance, Governance & Risk Management
items.description: Build audit-ready programs covering PIPEDA, PHIPA, OSFI, SOC 2, and sector-specific obligations.

items.title: Continuous Monitoring & Improvement
items.description: Metrics, penetration testing, threat intel integration, and iterative hardening — security is not a project.

industries.name: Financial Services
industries.description: OSFI Cyber Security Guideline B-13 compliant programs, operational resilience, and third-party risk.

industries.name: Healthcare
industries.description: PHIPA-compliant security programs protecting clinical data and medical devices.

industries.name: Government & Public Sector
industries.description: ITSG-33 aligned security controls for Protected A/B/C classifications.

items: Cloud security (AWS Security Hub, Azure Defender, GCP SCC)
Frameworks: ISO 27001, NIST CSF, SOC 2, CIS, ITSG-33

businessImpact.title: Business Impact
businessImpact.description: Our cybersecurity solutions are designed to deliver measurable risk reduction.
items: Reduced risk of cyberattacks and data breaches
Stronger protection of critical assets and data
Increased customer trust and brand reputation

points.title: Risk-Based, Not Checkbox
points.description: We start with business risk — then design controls proportional to it. Not every organization needs the same security program.

points.title: Canadian Regulatory Depth
points.description: OSFI B-13, PIPEDA, PHIPA, ITSG-33 — we navigate the Canadian compliance landscape your auditors and regulators actually assess against.

points.title: Engineering + Security
points.description: We bring software engineering discipline to security: IaC for controls, automated compliance, and DevSecOps practices.

points.title: Board-Ready Risk Communication
points.description: We translate technical risk into language executives and boards can act on — no FUD, just clear trade-offs.

caseStudy.title: Ransomware-Ready in 90 Days
caseStudy.challenge: After witnessing neighboring municipalities fall to ransomware, leadership demanded rapid improvement. Legacy endpoints, flat network, no SIEM, and an incident response plan no one had tested.
caseStudy.approach: We prioritized by risk: deployed EDR across all endpoints, segmented the network, stood up a managed SIEM/SOAR, and ran 3 tabletop exercises. Hardened backups with immutable storage and isolation.
caseStudy.result: Within 90 days: 100% EDR coverage, ransomware-resilient backups, 24/7 threat monitoring, tested IR playbook. Cyber insurance premium reduced 25% with improved posture documentation.

items.question: Do we need to be SOC 2 certified?
items.answer: It depends on your customers. If you sell to US enterprises, SOC 2 is often required. If you're serving Canadian FIs or healthcare, ISO 27001 or alignment to OSFI/PHIPA may be more relevant. We help evaluate which certifications actually matter for your business.

items.question: What's the difference between compliance and security?
items.answer: Compliance is a floor, not a ceiling. You can be PIPEDA-compliant and still be hacked. Real security starts with understanding your actual threats and building controls that matter. Compliance typically follows naturally from good security.

items.question: How do you help with incident response?
items.answer: We build IR capabilities in three stages: (1) plan + runbooks + tabletop exercises; (2) SIEM/SOAR detection and response tooling; (3) retainer-based on-call support for incidents. Most clients need the first two; some opt for the retainer.

items.question: What about insider threats?
items.answer: Insider threats (malicious or negligent) are often overlooked. We address them with least-privilege IAM, PAM for admins, user behavior analytics (UEBA), DLP, and security awareness training tailored to your workforce.

items.question: How do you keep up with evolving threats?
items.answer: Continuous monitoring, threat intelligence integration, regular penetration testing, and active participation in the Canadian security community (Canadian Centre for Cyber Security guidance, CISO Executive Network, OWASP, etc.).

closing.title: Ready to strengthen your security posture and protect your business?
closing.subtitle: Let's build a cybersecurity strategy tailored to your risks, regulations, and goals.

caseStudy.title: From Spreadsheet Chaos to Executive Clarity
caseStudy.challenge: Monthly board packages were compiled manually from 15+ spreadsheets, took 5 analysts a full week, and still contained errors. KPIs from different teams never matched.
caseStudy.approach: We built a governed data model on the existing data warehouse, established a KPI catalog with executive sign-off, and delivered Power BI dashboards with drill-through to operational detail. Trained analysts on dbt and the semantic layer.
caseStudy.result: Board package generation time reduced from 5 days to 2 hours. Analysts reclaimed 30+ hours/week for actual analysis. Executive team reports 'finally trusting the numbers'.

items.question: Which BI tool should we use?
items.answer: It depends on your team's skills, existing licenses, data platform, and use cases. Power BI integrates well with Microsoft stacks; Tableau excels at visualization; Looker offers strong governance; Qlik has a loyal analyst base. We help evaluate honestly.

items.question: How do you prevent dashboard sprawl?
items.answer: We establish a governed catalog of certified dashboards with clear ownership, regular usage reviews, and retirement of unused content. Self-service still requires guardrails.

items.question: What's a semantic layer and why does it matter?
items.answer: A semantic layer (dbt metrics, LookML, Cube) defines KPIs and metrics once — so 'revenue' means the same thing whether calculated in Power BI, Tableau, a SQL query, or an alert. It's the foundation of trust in BI.

items.question: Can you help migrate from legacy BI tools?
items.answer: Yes. We've migrated from Cognos, BusinessObjects, MicroStrategy, and custom reporting platforms to modern BI tools — focusing on re-implementing the 20% of reports that actually drive decisions.

items.question: How do you measure BI success?
items.answer: Adoption metrics (active users, session frequency), business outcomes (decisions made faster, manual reporting reduced), and qualitative feedback from the teams using the dashboards. Not just 'we delivered X dashboards'.

closing.title: Ready to transform your data into insights that drive results?
closing.subtitle: Let's build a BI solution that your team will actually use and trust.

hero.headline: Process massive datasets at speed and scale
hero.subheadline: When your data volumes outgrow traditional databases, you need architecture designed for scale. We build distributed data platforms that handle petabytes, stream millions of events, and power AI workloads without breaking.

challenge.title: Your data outgrew your infrastructure
challenge.description: Transactional databases weren't built for petabyte-scale analytics. Traditional warehouses struggle with unstructured data, streaming events, and the compute demands of modern AI workloads.
painPoints: Analytical queries on transactional databases slow down operations
Batch ETL jobs can't keep up with data arrival rates
Unstructured data (documents, images, logs) sits unused in cheap storage
AI and ML initiatives stall because training data is everywhere but nowhere
Scaling traditional data warehouses gets expensive fast

differentiators.title: Why Gamma Neutral for Big Data

points.title: Open Standards First
points.description: We build on open formats (Parquet, Iceberg, Delta) and open engines (Spark, Trino) — avoiding the vendor lock-in that kills long-term agility.

points.title: Cost-Aware Architecture
points.description: Big data doesn't have to mean big bills. We engineer for storage/compute separation, intelligent tiering, and workload-appropriate infrastructure.

points.title: Production Operations
points.description: We don't stop at architecture — we implement observability, auto-scaling, and operational runbooks for reliable production systems.

points.title: AI-Ready Foundations
points.description: Our platforms are designed with AI/ML workloads in mind: feature stores, vector databases, and distributed training infrastructure.

caseStudy.title: From Warehouse Bottleneck to Lakehouse Scale
caseStudy.challenge: Legacy data warehouse couldn't handle 10TB/day of network telemetry. Nightly ETL jobs ran 14+ hours, analysts waited until noon for yesterday's data, and fraud detection was reactive not real-time.
caseStudy.approach: Architected a lakehouse on Databricks with Kafka streaming for real-time ingestion, Delta Lake for storage, and Structured Streaming for continuous fraud detection. Implemented medallion architecture and Unity Catalog for governance.
caseStudy.result: Data freshness improved from 14 hours to 90 seconds. Fraud detection shifted from batch-reactive to real-time preventive. Infrastructure cost reduced 45% vs. legacy warehouse scaling path.

items.question: When do we actually need 'big data' architecture?
items.answer: When traditional databases/warehouses struggle with your volume, velocity, or variety. Often this is > a few TB of data, > millions of events/day, or mixing structured with unstructured data. Don't over-architect for data you don't have yet.

items.question: Spark vs. Databricks vs. EMR — what's the difference?
items.answer: Spark is the open-source engine. Databricks is a managed platform built around Spark with notebooks, Delta Lake, and governance. EMR/Dataproc/HDInsight are cloud-provider managed Spark services. Databricks typically has the richest ecosystem; provider services are often cheaper.

items.question: What's a lakehouse and how is it different from a data lake?
items.answer: Traditional data lakes store raw files in object storage — cheap but no ACID transactions, schema enforcement, or fast queries. Lakehouses (Delta, Iceberg, Hudi) add warehouse-like features to the data lake, enabling both streaming and batch on the same storage layer.

items.question: How do you handle data governance at scale?
items.answer: Catalog-first approach: Unity Catalog, AWS Glue Catalog, or equivalent for metadata. Fine-grained access (row/column level), PII discovery and masking, lineage tracking end-to-end, and audit logging wired into observability.

items.question: Can big data platforms be cost-effective?
items.answer: Absolutely — but it requires discipline. Storage/compute separation, tiered storage lifecycle policies, query optimization, workload isolation, and rightsizing clusters. Without FinOps practice, big data becomes expensive data fast.

closing.title: Ready to unlock insights from your massive datasets?
closing.subtitle: Let's design a Big Data solution that scales with your business needs — and your budget.

about.intro: Gamma Neutral was founded on a simple observation: too many organizations invest in data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology without the engineering discipline to make it work. Our team brings hands-on experience from financial services, government, and healthcare technology programs — and we built this firm to deliver the focused, senior-level attention that large consultancies cannot.

pointOfView.title: Our Point of View
pointOfView.content: We believe that the gap between a promising AI prototype and a production-ready system is where most data initiatives fail. Gamma Neutral exists to close that gap — with rigorous engineering, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a commitment to solutions your team can actually maintain after we leave.

values.partnershipDesc: Your team succeeds after we leave
